title: "false" UNOPT warnings
body:
Sometimes usage of complex type variables ( collections of wires) causes UNOPT warnings . Seems verilator treats these as single object. This happens to vectors and packed structures. Other lint tools tolerate such constructs. 
This is pretty annoying problem, causing these warnings be disabled completely.

For ex:

```
module top(
input [10:0] in,
output [12:0] out
);


assign out[10:0] = in;
assign out[12:11] = {2{out[10]}};

endmodule
```

causes following warning:

```
%Warning-UNOPT: noopt.sv:11:15: Signal unoptimizable: Feedback to public clock or circular logic: 'out'
   11 | output [12:0] out
      |               ^~~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off UNOPT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
                noopt.sv:11:15:      Example path: out
                noopt.sv:25:19:      Example path: ASSIGNW
                noopt.sv:11:15:      Example path: out
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

 Similar warnings happen if a structure member depends on another structure member via some other variables assignments,
sometimes in other modules
